reviews_list.batch_info: Diploma in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2021
reviews_list.review_title: Our college provided the best placements for electrical engineering in 2021.

review_content.Placements: More than 90% of students get placed. The salary package offered was not good, but the companies were good for our core branch. The minimum salary package was 2.6 LPA and the highest salary package was 6.2 LPA for a diploma student. Some top recruiting companies are Radiant Electronics, Wipro, and so on.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities were very good. All the students were allowed to use Wi-Fi, but some of the sites, like Instagram and Facebook, couldn't be accessed. Labs and infrastructure were above average. We had 2 hostels with 4 floors each, one for girls and the other for boys. They provided good facilities.

review_content.Placements: So many students got placements. I suggest students take admission to this college. There are so many good opportunities that will help us build a career in a good field. reviews_list.review_title: Dissatisfied because the facilities are not good.

review_content.Placements: There are 26 students in my course. Honestly, I don't have any idea about it. I think 60% of students got internships in companies like NDTV. Someone joined as a music teacher in a school, and some students went to study higher.
review_content.Infrastructure: There are ACs that sometimes work well and sometimes not. There is Wi-Fi, but students don't have access to it for the past two years, even after paying for it. The classrooms are average, and the library is good. I don't live in the hostel, so I don't know about it.

review_content.Placements: Great placement. 70% of students get placed rest go for higher studies. Students are guided for various jobs opportunities by professors. Hostel for both boys and girls is there with excellent service. Many canteens are there like Hush, Chaingaram, subway, hotshot, hungercure etc. Medical service is also available.

reviews_list.review_title: The college has a fleet of luxury A/C buses to provide transport.

review_content.Placements: Institute has been able to break the barriers of declining placements due to global and national economic meltdown and recession in the last few years and attracted multinational and Indian companies for campus placements. We have witnessed more than 150 companies.

review_content.Placements: 90% of students are placed in my course the highest and the lowest average package offered in my course is 20,00,000 annually and top recruiting companies of my course the students are placed is Google and the 95% of students got Internship in the first year only and highly approved companies.

The quality of the hostel food in the mess and canteen is good and sports in our colleges at high-level.

reviews_list.review_title: The college has good infrastructure and I got very previous teachers from here.

paragraphs: School of Media Studies and Humanities MRIIRS admission processinvolves registration, selection or appearing for entrance exam and admission confirmation. Candidates can fill out the application form on the official website of MRIIRS. Below are the steps to get a seat in School of Media Studies and Humanities:

Aspirants fulfilling the eligibility requirements can apply for the preferred course. Candidates have to pay an application fee ofINR 1,200and upload the specified documents to submit the application form.
Step 2: Selection

Aspirants are shortlisted for admission based on their merit in the last qualifying exam and MRNAT score. Aspirants with a valid score in CUET-PG can get admission in the MA programme. The institute releases theMRNAT resulton the official website. Further, MRIIRS also releases merit list to announce the names of the shortlisted candidates.
Step 3: Acceptance of Admission & Payment of Fee

Step 4: Physical Verification of Documents & Enrollment Process

All the aspirants who have accepted the admission offer have to report at the campus for document verification and enrollment process. Further, candidates need to carry all the relevant documents and admission offer letter and original fee receipts with them for verification.

content.Placements: Students should score 60-70% above in academics to become eligible for placements. The lowest package is 2 LPA and the average package offered ranges from 5-6 LPA. The university successfully conducts placement drives with companies like Amazon, Wipro, TCS, Accenture, etc.

highlights.summary:
NSDC, or the National Skill Development Corporation, is a prominent catalyst in India’s skill development landscape. It functions as a facilitator through partnership funding within a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model, and it’s under the Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (MSDE).

This large skill ecosystem does not directly operate as a course provider, but forms partnerships and implements various government initiatives. NSDC has accredited training centres and partners all over India to empower Indian youth, basing its offerings through evaluating latest market demands.

While the official NSDC website opens doors to apprenticeship and lab-based vocational training, it enables online learning for the broader Skill India Mission through Skill India Digital Hub under the section labelled as NSDC Academy.

Some of the certifications from here are defined under the well-defined National Skills Qualifications Framework that lead to employment opportunities.

Also, it supports various schemes such as PMKVY, PMKK, and TITP, through which it has been equally helping the underserved population and urban jobseekers with employability skills since 2008.

highlights.summary:
Vskills is one of India’s most prominent certification bodies for 30+ disciplines, including project management, marketing, and cloud computing. This Cubezoid Solutions Pvt. Ltd.-managed platform aims to improve employability by offering updated in-demand government certifications that help one stand out in today’s competitive job market.

The government certification courses on Vskills are from the ICSIL, an Indian Government Body, which is also a JV of Govt of NCT Delhi (DSIIDC) and Govt of India (TCIL).

The courses from Vskills come with lifetime learner access and job support, making them a uniquely affordable solution for working professionals. It also stands out as an all-in-one platform for finding updated certifications, taking practice exams, and reissuing certificates, as most require one to update annually. The platform also newly features a job assessment test that’s useful for employees and companies.

Vskills also has third-party assessment programmes in partnership with Whizlabs, Digital Vidya, and Getcertgo for sought-after certifications, including Six Sigma, SEO, data analytics, and human resources.

highlights.summary:
Interaction Design Foundation, aka IxDF, is among the best value-for-money online design schools. It focuses on self-paced, community-based, and personalised learning with real-expert-graded courses about everything UX.

The certificates from IxDF hold good value in today’s global UX job market, which demands customer-centricity, innovation, and interdisciplinarity. IxDF’s portfolio covers 37 beginner to advanced-level courses with role-based learning paths.

This educational foundation has 213 design experts developing the curricula, which has helped 177,800+ learners and around 1380+ organisations since 2002. Brands such as Adobe and SAP use this online membership platform to train their UX design teams, while universities, including MIT and Imperial College of London, use its course materials.

highlights.summary:
openSAP was the free Enterprise MOOC platform for learning SAP solutions. The offerings have migrated to the SAP Learning site after July 2024.

While openSAP’s 100+ courses are now integrated into the new platform, the site has yet to migrate the Microlearnings and Podcasts between early- and mid-2025. These two will remain on the openSAP website until further official notice.

openSAP’s courses are free and self-paced. These remain the same on the SAP Learning site. The latter also has a subscription-based plan that includes exam attempts, which openSAP did not have. Other than that, all new courses on SAP technology and solutions are available on the SAP Learning Hub. Do refer to SAP courses and certifications.

faqs.answer: openSAP had the traditional MOOC structure, while SAP Learning is more modular with simulations. The learning is more immersive in SAP Learning but does not have discussion forums like on openSAP. The former platform also provided a Record of Achievement, while the latter does not with many courses.
faqs.question: Q: How are openSAP and SAP Learning Hub different?

faqs.answer: openSAP has Microlearning and Podcasts. They are yet to be transferred to SAP Learning. Currently, you can access these two individual platforms of openSAP.
faqs.question: Q: How do you access other learning platforms of openSAP?

faqs.answer: There are no special requirements to access openSAP offerings with its Microlearning modules and Podcasts. You just have to register using the respective portals. On SAP Learning, you will have to choose an annual subscription. There are free learning journeys as well. But some openSAP courses will require some basic knowledge of the SAP ecosystem.

faq_section.heading: FAQs
faq_section.introduction: openSAP courses were designed to be self-paced and free with structured guides, discussion forums, ad Record of Achievement to learning SAP technologies and concepts. These courses have been integrated with SAP Learning Hub, which offers a more centralised and modular learning structure for newcomers.

answer.text: openSAP had the traditional MOOC structure, while SAP Learning is more modular with simulations. The learning is more immersive in SAP Learning but does not have discussion forums like on openSAP. The former platform also provided a Record of Achievement, while the latter does not with many courses.

faqs.question: How are openSAP and SAP Learning Hub different?

answer.text: openSAP has Microlearning and Podcasts. They are yet to be transferred to SAP Learning. Currently, you can access these two individual platforms of openSAP.
